title: Moltkiopsis ciliata
taxonomy: Moltkiopsis ciliata is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. The plant is native to Europe and Asia and belongs to the order Lamiales. Moltkiopsis ciliata is commonly known as the hairy-leaved gromwell. It is a low-growing, perennial herb with hairy leaves and small, blue or purple flowers. The plant is often found in dry, rocky areas and can thrive in a variety of soil conditions.
uses: Moltkiopsis ciliata, also known as rock rose, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and stems contain flavonoids and other compounds with potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Moltkiopsis ciliata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia due to its adaptogenic properties.
